The code excerpt ends abruptly with 'if' and doesn't show the full implementation.
    Why it dropped: The current iteration appears to be truncated or incomplete, showing only partial functions instead of the complete implementation. This makes it impossible to evaluate the full API design, input flexibility, and complete type hint coverage that was present in the best version.

This makes the error handling incomplete since the full try/except patterns and function completions are missing, reducing the robustness score.

While the scorer mentions 'excellent test coverage', no actual test examples are visible in the provided excerpt, suggesting the test cases that earned points in iteration 2 may have been removed or are in the missing portion.
    → RECOVER: Complete the truncated implementation by restoring all missing functions from the best version (especially `detect_headers` and the complete `_fallback_delimiter_detection`), and ensure all test examples that were present in iteration 2 are included to maintain the test_examples score component.

  FIX 7: code_api_design.type_hints (current: 50%, 2.8 pts at stake)

  FIX 9: code_robustness.return_metadata (current: 50%, 2.5 pts at stake)

  FIX 10: code_testability.pure_function (current: 50%, 2.4 pts at stake)
